Airbnb URL Source: https://simple.wikipedia.org/wiki/Airbnb Published Time: 2021-05-14T12:10:53Z Markdown Content: From ", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "AirBNB at Kevin Warnock", "url": "https://kevinwarnock.com/tag/airbnb/", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: AirBNB at Kevin Warnock URL Source: https://kevinwarnock.com/tag/airbnb/ Markdown Content: [![Image 1: Jeff Clavier on left August 30 2012 at Berkeley Entrepreneurs Forum Photo by Kevin Warnock](http://kevinwarnock.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/03/Jeff-Clavier-on-left-August-30-2012-at-Berkeley-Entrepreneurs-Forum-Photo-by-Kevin-Warnock-e1363999786520.jpg)](http://kevinwarnock.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/03/Jeff-Clavier-on-left-August-30-2012-at-Berkeley-Entrepreneurs-Forum-Photo-by-Kevin-Warnock-e1363999786520.jpg) Jeff Clavier, left, August 30, 2012, at Berkeley Entrepreneurs Forum at University of California Berkeley I have learned that smoke and fire detectors almost always signal low battery condition in the early morning between midnight and five o\u2019clock. This wakes me up and forces me to replace batteries when I am sleepy. I suspect many people don\u2019t have spare batteries always on hand like I do, and simply take the batteries out until they can go to the store. This leaves the premises less well protected, and I suspect fires have started during this period and that people have died. The fix is so simple I can\u2019t believe I only thought of it today. Simply include a clock in all battery operated smoke, fire and carbon monoxide detectors. Have this clock powered by its own battery that can last a decade or more. Set this clock at the factory for the part of the world where the detectors will be sold. The low battery circuitry should consult this on board clock, and should suppress the chirping \u2018low battery\u2019 notification during typical sleeping hours, say from 10pm to 8am. The chirping can resume at 8am each day until the batteries are replaced. Alarms typically will chirp for months before they run out of power entirely, so the delay in chirping during the night will not cause a significant safety issue. The clock should be user resettable so the detector can be moved between time zones by the buyer, or set to accommodate unusual sleep schedules. I suspect the clock I am proposing costs no more than USD $.50 in bulk, and the feature that it will enable can be promoted to boost sales. I suspect such a detector will sell well because I suspect everyone with a detector has been awakened by their chirping. Chirping during waking hours is likely to be a measurable advance in fire and poisoning safety, since awake and alert people are less likely to make mistakes, like removing the batteries or putting in replacement batteries backwards. [![Image 2: Jeff Clavier's Facebook Wall as of March 22, 2013](http://kevinwarnock.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/03/Jeff-Clavier-Facebook-Wall-March-22-20131.jpg)](http://kevinwarnock.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/03/Jeff-Clavier-Facebook-Wall-March-22-20131.jpg) Jeff Clavier's Facebook Wall as of March 22, 2013 While the circuit designers are at it, they should program the detectors to keep track of the passing years and notify the user when the detector has reached its end of life, around ten years after manufac", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "AirBnb: The Growth Story You Didn\u2019t Know - GrowthHackers.com", "url": "https://growthhackers.com/growth-studies/airbnb/", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: AirBnb: The Growth Story You Didn\u2019t Know - GrowthHackers.com URL Source: https://growthhackers.com/growth-studies/airbnb/ Published Time: 2023-04-18T17:22:11+00:00 Markdown Content: In 2007, designers Brian Chesky and Joe Gebbia couldn\u2019t afford the rent on their San Francisco apartment. To make ends meet, they decided to turn their loft into a lodging space, but, as Gebbia explains: > \u201cWe didn\u2019t want to post on Craigslist because we felt it was too impersonal. Our entrepreneur instinct said \u2018build your own site.\u2019 So we did.\u201d[[1]](https://allentrepreneur.wordpress.com/2009/08/26/travel-like-a-human-with-joe-gebbia-co-founder-of-airbnb/) There was a design conference coming to town and hotel space was limited, so they set up a simple website with pictures of their their loft-turned-lodging space\u2014complete with three air mattresses on the floor and the promise of a home-cooked breakfast in the morning. This site got them their first three renters, each one paying $80, and after that first weekend they began receiving emails from people around the world asking when the site would be available for destinations like Buenos Aires, London, and Japan. Gebbia explains: The following spring, they enlisted former roommate and engineer Nathan Blecharczyk to help them get Airbed & Breakfast off the ground. They planned the launch around the Democratic National Convention in order to capitalize on the resulting lack of hotel space. Fast forward seven years, and Airbed and Breakfast is now Airbnb\u2014a household name that has surpassed industry legacy Hilton Hotels in nights booked. As of spring 2014, the platform had 10 million guests and 550,000 properties listed worldwide, along with a $10B valuation\u2014making Airbnb worth more than legacy players like Wyndham and Hyatt.[[2]](http://techcrunch.com/gallery/a-brief-history-of-airbnb/)The company has received $776.4M from investors like Y Combinator, Sequoia Capital, Keith Rabois, Andreessen Horowitz, Ashton Kutcher, Founders Fund, and TPG Growth in a total of seven funding rounds\u2014the last of which raised $500M alone.[[3]](http://www.crunchbase.com/organization/airbnb) ![Image 1](https://growthhackers.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/AirBnb-Guest-Growth-768x378.png) ![Image 2](https://growthhackers.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/AirBnb-Funding-Growth-1024x806.png) ![Image 3](https://growthhackers.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/04/AirBnb-Listings-Growth-1024x814.png) But how did a few air mattresses on the floor of a San Francisco loft become the most widely-used anecdote for startup growth hacking? ### **Early Growth** #### \u201cPure, Unadulterated Hustle\u201d in the Face of Initial Resistance As they were starting out in the summer of 2008, the founders needed a way to raise money. They bought a ton of cereal and designed special edition election-themed boxes, released that fall\u2014Obama O\u2019s and Cap\u2019n McCain\u2019s, which they sold at convention parties for $40 a box. They sold 500 boxes of each cereal, helping them to raise around ", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "Earnings call transcript: Airbnb Q2 2025 beats expectations,", "url": "https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-airbnb-q2-2025-beats-expectations-stock-dips-93CH-4175241", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] Airbnbhas setarevenue guidance of $4.02-$4.1billionfor Q3 2025, representing an 8-10% growth, building on its impressive 5-year revenue CAGR of ...", "relevance_score": 0.5}, {"title": "Airbnb Is Spending Millions of Dollars to Make Nightmares Go", "url": "http://www.sensibleendowment.com/entry.php/15499", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title: Airbnb Is Spending Millions of Dollars to Make Nightmares Go Away URL Source: http://www.sensibleendowment.com/entry.php/15499 Markdown Content: # Airbnb Is Spending Millions of Dollars to Make Nightmares Go Away | Sensible Endowment [The Dollop](http://www.sensibleendowment.com/go.php/ad/94/?misc=ebad871c0a564f3cfccbda81aaf73fb1&url=http://thedollop.libsyn.com/) - It's history, but funny. Usually. Mostly. [Subscriber Link - click here to make yours.](http://www.sensibleendowment.com/ads.php) Friday, 25 June 2021 [![Image 6](http://www.sensibleendowment.com/images/entry_thumbnails/c09ebd37141c87d6d6f01c34a23f23a4.png)](http://www.sensibleendowment.com/go.php/15499/?misc=069639031c2e54a74d636caeb0d864b1&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.bloomberg.com%2Fnews%2Ffeatures%2F2021-06-15%2Fairbnb-spends-millions-making-nightmares-at-live-anywhere-rentals-go-away) [Airbnb Is Spending Millions of Dollars to Make Nightmares Go Away](http://www.sensibleendowment.com/go.php/15499/?misc=069639031c2e54a74d636caeb0d864b1&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.bloomberg.com%2Fnews%2Ffeatures%2F2021-06-15%2Fairbnb-spends-millions-making-nightmares-at-live-anywhere-rentals-go-away) quote [When things go horribly wrong during a stay, the company?s secretive safety team jumps in to soothe guests and hosts, help families?and prevent PR disasters.] Airbnb's city scape as a backdrop [**Reveal**](http://www.sensibleendowment.com/entry.php/15499 \"Show\") The first-floor apartment on West 37th Street, a few blocks south of Times Square, was popular with tourists?so popular that a set of keys was left at the counter of a nearby bodega for Airbnb renters to pick up. That?s where a 29-year-old Australian woman and a group of her friends retrieved them, no identification needed, when they arrived in Manhattan to celebrate New Year?s Eve in 2015. The apartment had been advertised on Airbnb even though most short-term rentals are illegal in New York. The city, prodded by powerful hotel unions, was at war with the company, which was listing thousands of apartments in the five boroughs despite some of the strictest regulations in the country. Soon after ringing in the new year, the woman left her friends at the bar where they?d been celebrating and returned to the apartment on her own. She didn?t notice anything amiss or see the man standing in the shadows as she walked into the bathroom. By the time she realized she wasn?t alone, the blade of a kitchen knife was pointing down at her. The stranger grabbed her, shoved her onto a bed, and raped her. Drunken revelers were wandering the streets outside, but the woman was too scared to scream. The attacker fled with her phone, but she managed to reach her friends with an iPad, and they ran into the street to find a police officer. The cops were already in the apartment an hour or so later when the man returned and peered into the doorway. They caught him and emptied his backpack, pulling out three incriminating items: a knife, one of the woman?s earrings, an", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "Airbnb, Inc. (NASDAQ:ABNB) Q4 2024 Earnings Call Transcript -", "url": "https://www.insidermonkey.com/blog/airbnb-inc-nasdaqabnb-q4-2024-earnings-call-transcript-1453730/", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] At the end of the year, we had $10.6billionof corporate cash and investments,aswellas$5.9billionof funds held on behalf of our guests.", "relevance_score": 0.5}, {"title": "Timeline of Airbnb - Timelines", "url": "https://timelines.issarice.com/wiki/Timeline_of_Airbnb", "snippet": "[Jina Extracted Content] Title:  URL Source: https://timelines.issarice.com/wiki/Timeline_of_Airbnb Markdown Content: Verifying that you are a human...
